g.text: <think>
</think>
Mode 1: Self-Monitoring / Reflection (Reflection)
“Wait, my previous assumption (that the denominator is nonzero) may
not hold under the boundary conditions. I need to re-check the interval range.”
Function:
Demonstrates the model's ability to re-evaluate its
own reasoning process.
Mode 2: Self-Check / Verification (Verification)
“Substitute x = 2 into the left-hand side of the original equation:
4 + 2 = 6; substitute into the right-hand side: 3 × 2 = 6.
Both sides are equal, so the stepwise conclusion is correct.”
Function:
Demonstrates the model actively seeking Ground Truth
signals to support its reasoning chain and prevent the accumulation of errors.
Mode 3: Backtracking / Correction (Backtrack)
“Based on the previous verification, the algebraic method is too
complicated for solving this geometry problem. I should go back
to step 3 and try the auxiliary-line approach.”
Function:
A hallmark behavior that distinguishes Long-CoT from
ordinary CoT, showing the model's resilience in exploring alternative
paths when it reaches a 'dead end'.
